market_notes: Iran war de-escalation inflection (Trump signals 2-3 week resolution) triggered broad equity rally and oil retreat from >$100; risk-on tone accelerating flight-to-quality rotation into mega-cap growth and semiconductor infrastructure. SPY consolidating after Q1 weakness, QQQ/SMH rebounding. NVIDIA $2B Marvell partnership announced 1h ago validates AI data center networking TAM expansion and enterprise capex cycle durability. ASML endorsed by European government AI infrastructure backing (announced 3h ago). Semiconductor equipment cycle remains barometer for AI capex cycle health; capitulation risk low but near-term volatility elevated. Fed guidance pending—critical inflection point for growth repricing. Geopolitical risk elevated but de-escalation narrative reducing macro tail risk. Portfolio redeployed from 82% to ~78% cash with 5 new positions targeting AI infrastructure, equipment cycle, and distributed cloud compute beneficiaries.
watching: TSEM breakout above $175 resistance—confirms resumption of parabolic uptrend and foundry capex cycle acceleration
MRVL breakout above $100 resistance—validates NVIDIA partnership execution and data center networking TAM expansion
NVDA technical retest of $180 zone—mega-cap quality rotation continuation signal and potential relief bounce target
AMAT semiconductor equipment order guidance (quarterly commentary)—validates AI capex cycle durability and foundry capacity utilization
Federal Reserve communications and rate guidance—macro inflection point for growth repricing and recession risk assessment
ASML order book and earnings guidance (April guidance cycle)—leading indicator for semiconductor capex cycle confidence
Oil price persistence >$100 and Iran escalation risk—macro headwind for growth equities if de-escalation narrative breaks
META relief bounce target $540–550—potential short re-entry signal if technicals confirm legal verdict digestion
ARM earnings guidance on AI/CPU IP licensing momentum—validates SoftBank/OpenAI synergy and foundational infrastructure thesis
Semiconductor equipment orders data (KLAC earnings, ASML guidance)—validates AI capex cycle durability and institutional confidence
SPY support at $580–600 range—macro capitulation vs. grinding lower scenario; portfolio risk assessment level
GlobalFoundries patent suit vs TSEM evolution—legal/competitive risk monitor for foundry positioning in advanced node capex cycle

recent_runs.reasoning: Portfolio is appropriately positioned in high-conviction AI infrastructure beneficiaries (ARM, MRVL, TSEM, AKAM, ASML) with META short hedge intact. Tech correction remains structural with flight-to-quality favoring semiconductor/networking names. No urgent catalysts require new entries; TSEM breakout above $175 and MRVL above $95 remain key technical confirmation levels. META short thesis strengthened by fresh legal verdict on youth engagement design. Holding 79% cash is justified given macro uncertainty (Fed guidance pending, recession signals mixed) and elevated volatility. Current setup captures asymmetric risk/reward in quality oversold names without forced deployment.

**Macro:** Markets rallied strongly on Iran war de-escalation signal (Trump claims resolution in 2-3 weeks); oil retreated from >$100, risk-on tone returned. SPY flat 1W but Q1 close positive; QQQ and SMH rebounding after corrections. Flight-to-quality thesis intact but immediate macro tailwind reducing defensive urgency. Fed signals still pending—key inflection point. **Sectors:** Tech rotation accelerating into quality mega-caps (NVDA, ARM surging on AI optimism); semiconductor/infrastructure beneficiaries outperforming broader correction. Geopolitical relief lift broadening beyond quality into cyclicals (energy, financials). **Positions:** Portfolio is 82% cash, heavily underdeployed given rally + technical breakouts. ARM +12.1% 1W, MRVL +7.2% 1W, TSEM consolidating near $175 resistance are all tracking thesis. AKAM flat but thesis intact. ASML down -5.6% 1W but order book strength remains secular tailwind. No urgent exits; market inflection suggests incremental deployment opportunity into validated names + new entry setup in beaten-down AI infrastructure. Recent META short closure realized 8.8% loss but freed capital for offensive positioning.
